name: codevetter-warm-local-qualification
description: Representative qualification corpus for a warm local verifier. This fixture describes coverage and is not the final runtime scenario contract.

scenarios.intent: Usage totals and charts respond coherently to range and provider filters.
assertions: the displayed total matches the deterministic seven-day fixture
the provider series and daily detail agree

scenarios.intent: A developer can narrow recent sessions and inspect the selected result.
interactions: return to the complete session list
assertions: only deterministic failed sessions appear while filtered
the selected session detail preserves its provider and timestamp

scenarios.intent: A degraded source can be refreshed without corrupting the other source summaries.
assertions: exactly one refresh mutation is recorded
the refreshed source becomes healthy while unrelated counts stay stable

scenarios.intent: The Review workspace preserves change-source and file selection correctly.
interactions: switch from worktree changes to staged changes
switch back to the first changed file
assertions: the changed-file list reflects the selected source
the code pane and file metadata refer to the same file

scenarios.intent: Finding filters and resolution state remain synchronized with the active finding.
assertions: the active finding has high severity
exactly one resolution mutation occurs and the queue count decrements

scenarios.intent: A verification target can be edited and restored without starting a model-driven run.
assertions: the route field preserves each deterministic edit
no verification or model-provider mutation is made

scenarios.intent: Stored verification evidence can be inspected and copied from Review.
assertions: the expanded evidence belongs to the selected review
the copied summary contains the fixture outcome and provenance

scenarios.intent: Repository inventory filters expose the right deterministic files and summaries.
assertions: only TypeScript fixture files remain after filtering
the opened source belongs to the selected language group

scenarios.intent: Commit history selection updates its file and verification evidence consistently.
interactions: select the second commit in history
assertions: commit metadata and changed files come from the same fixture commit
the evidence provenance points to that commit

scenarios.intent: Selecting and filtering a graph node presents a consistent local neighborhood.
assertions: the details panel identifies the selected node
every displayed relationship is present in the deterministic graph fixture

scenarios.intent: Walking repository history reconstructs the graph at stable release boundaries.
interactions: move the history position from latest to the middle release
select a node added in the middle release
move back to the latest release
assertions: the middle release graph contains its added node and exact edge count
returning to latest restores the latest graph identity

scenarios.intent: Quality-risk ranking can be narrowed and traced back to source evidence.
assertions: the selected risk is the highest-ranked qualifying fixture
the source evidence path and risk explanation remain visible

scenarios.intent: Agent run status filters lead to the correct deterministic run detail.
assertions: the selected run is completed and has the expected adapter
the output summary belongs to the selected run

scenarios.intent: Cancelling an active local run requires confirmation and emits only one mutation.
assertions: exactly one cancellation mutation is recorded
the run moves from running to cancelled

scenarios.intent: Local watcher configuration accepts valid polling and branch inputs.
interactions: set the poll interval to five seconds
set the base branch to main
assertions: the normalized configuration matches the deterministic inputs
no watcher starts before explicit confirmation

scenarios.intent: A developer can filter watcher events and inspect a failed event safely.
assertions: only failed fixture events appear while filtered
the diagnostic detail preserves the event identity and changed paths

scenarios.intent: Provider settings validate editable metadata without persisting credentials.
interactions: replace it with the deterministic valid URL
assertions: invalid input produces an inline validation message
valid input clears the message without issuing a save mutation

scenarios.intent: Rubric filtering and preview preserve the selected rubric's content.
assertions: the selected rubric belongs to the TypeScript fixture set
the preview contains its deterministic rule count

scenarios.intent: Local memory search returns the correct evidence-backed detail.
assertions: the deterministic graph-history observation is returned
its source metadata and timestamp match the fixture

scenarios.intent: Global keyboard navigation reaches a direct destination without stale page state.
interactions: open the command palette with the platform shortcut
assertions: the final route is /settings
the Settings page is visible and the command palette is closed
